title: pkcs11 put: destroy-then-create is non-atomic, loses secret on failure
description: Trigger: any failure (HSM disconnection, process crash, power loss, network interruption) between the destroy_object() loop and the create_object() call in SecretStore::put. Bad behavior: the existing CKO_DATA object is permanently destroyed before the new one is created. If create_object() fails or never runs, the secret is gone from the HSM with no recovery path. PKCS11 does not support atomic replace, so the fix requires a different ordering: create the new object first under a temporary label, verify success, then destroy the old object and rename — or at minimum document that put is destructive and non-recoverable on failure.
